name: qiskit-qasm3-import

metadata.name: qiskit-qasm3-import
metadata.summary: Importer for parsing OpenQASM 3 strings into Qiskit circuits

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.physical_qubit_index
documentation: If this name is a physical qubit, return its integer index. If not, return `None`.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.add_dummy_parameter_reference
documentation: Ensure that a circuit contains at least one reference to a given parameter.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.AddressingMode

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.AddressingMode.set_physical_mode
documentation:
Set the addressing mode to physical. On success return `True`, otherwise raise an
exception.

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.AddressingMode.set_virtual_mode
documentation:
Set the addressing mode to virtual.
On success return `True`, otherwise raise an exception.

Addressing mode for qubits in OpenQASM 3 programs.

This class is useful as long as we allow only physical or virtual addressing modes, but not
mixed. If the latter is supported in the future, this class will be modified or removed.

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.SymbolTables.globals
documentation: Return an iterator over the global symbols.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.State

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.State.new_with_local_scope
documentation: Return a copy of `context` modified to include a new local scope on the stack.

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.State.new_with_gate_scope
documentation: Return a copy of `context` modified to include a new gate scope on the stack.

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.state.State.unique_name
documentation: Get a name that is not defined in the current scope.

Mutable state used during translation of OpenQASM code to a QuantumCircuit.

Slots:
  scope (Scope): The current lexical scope for the statements being translated.
  _source (str): The entire OpenQASM program being translated. This is not directly
              translated, but rather an AST derived from the source is the input.
              Instead, this source is used for diagnostics.
  circuit (qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit): The output of the translation.
  symbol_table (SymbolTables): A structure that tracks the symbols (e.g. identifiers) that
              have been encountered along with some information about them.
  _unique (function) : A function that returns unique symbol names.
  addressing_mode: A structure that tracks the state of the addressing mode; either unknown,
              virtual, or hardware.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.exceptions.raise_from_node
documentation: Raise a {exc}`.ConversionError` caused by the given `node`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.exceptions.ConversionError
documentation:
Raised when an error occurs converting from the AST representation into a
{class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit`. This is often due to OpenQASM 3 constructs that have
no equivalent in Qiskit.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.api.convert
documentation:
Convert a parsed OpenQASM 3 program in AST form, into a Qiskit
{class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit`.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.api.parse
documentation:
Wrapper around {func}`.convert`, which first parses the OpenQASM 3 program into AST form, and
then converts the output to Qiskit format.

modules.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.expression
documentation:
QASM visitors for the expression tree, resolving it into objects that are useful for interaction
with Qiskit.

Ideally, this would just have a single large `ExpressionResolver` or something like that, but
since Qiskit's type system can't represent anywhere close to what OpenQASM 3 can represent, the
output types would be rather confusing. For now, it's much easier to split into a handful of more
specialised resolvers that can bail out early when they encounter things that are outside their
scope, rather than trying to build a whole new internal IR to handle everything at once.
This code is part of Qiskit.
(C) Copyright IBM 2024.
This code is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. You may obtain a copy of this license
in the LICENSE.txt file in the root directory of this source tree or at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.
Any modifications or derivative works of this code must retain this copyright notice, and modified
files need to carry a notice indicating that they have been altered from the originals.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.expression.resolve_condition
documentation:
A resolver for conditions that can be converted into Qiskit's very basic equality form
of either `Clbit == bool` or `ClassicalRegister == int`.

This effectively just handles very special outer cases, then delegates the rest of the work to a
{class}`.ValueResolver`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.expression.ValueResolver

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.expression.ValueResolver.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=no-self-use

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.expression.ValueResolver.resolve
documentation:
The entry point to the resolver, resolving the AST node into a 2-tuple of a relevant
Qiskit type, and the {class}`.Type` that it is an instance of.

A resolver for value-like objects that have exact Qiskit representations. Everything handled
here should resolve into a single Qiskit object (or occasionally a compound of such objects,
like lists of bits).

These resolvers are intended to be short-lived. Their instance state is the instance of
{class}`.State` created in {meth}`.ConvertVisitor.convert()`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Type

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Type.pretty
documentation: A pretty string representation of the type, useful for debugging.

An internal representation of the OpenQASM 3 type system values, or at least that parts of it
that Terra has some sort of support for. The reference AST does not have a nice unified object
to use here; there is {class}`~openqasm3.ast.ClassicalType` but no quantum equivalent (since
it's implicit).

This class is just a typing base, and should never be instantiated. Its subclasses will be,
though.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Error
documentation: A zero type that represents an error during type checking.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Never
documentation:
The bottom type.  There are no valid values of this type, as it can never be instantiated.
This is used during inference in cases where multiple types must combined into their join, but
some of the elements have missing values, such as a range that has a start but no stop value.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.BitArray
documentation:
An array of bits. This roughly corresponds to Terra's
{class}`~qiskit.circuit.ClassicalRegister`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.QubitArray
documentation:
An array of qubits. This roughly corresponds to Terra's
{class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumRegister`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Bit
documentation: A single bit. This corresponds to Terra's {class}`~qiskit.circuit.Clbit`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Bool
documentation: A Boolean value.  This is only used by Terra in for single-bit conditions.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Qubit
documentation: A single qubit. This corresponds to Terra's {class}`~qiskit.circuit.Qubit`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.HardwareQubit
documentation:
A hardware qubit.
This corresponds a hardware qubits referenced in Terra's {class}`~qiskit.transpiler.TranspilerLayout`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Int
documentation:
An integer value. This is generally only encountered as a constant and so is represented by
a Python integer, but can also be the type of the Qiskit {class}`~qiskit.circuit.Parameter` used
to represent `for`-loop variables.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Uint
documentation: An unsigned integer value.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Float
documentation:
A floating-point type. Terra can use this either in a constant form as a Python `float`,
or as a {class}`~qiskit.circuit.Parameter`.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Angle
documentation:
An angle type. OpenQASM 3 makes a large distinction between `angle` and `float` (the
OpenQASM angle is integer-like), but Terra currently treats them as interchangeable. This might
change in the future.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Duration
documentation:
A duration. Right now, this is only recognised in constant form, which is represented within
{class}`.ValueResolver` as a 2-tuple of a float and its unit.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Range
documentation: A range selector.  The inner type is the join of the types of the start and end values.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Sequence
documentation:
A general sequence of values.  This is represented internally as a list or tuple of the
contained type.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.types.Gate
documentation:
The type of a gate.  Since the classical parameters of gates have a fixed type in OpenQASM 3,
this just stores the counts of the classical and quantum arguments.

functions.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.converter._escape_qasm2
documentation:
Escape a `name` to produce a valid OpenQASM 2 identifier (ignoring things like reserved
keywords).  This is necessary for registers as of Terra 0.22 beacuse their names have an
initialisation check that they match this regex.  It should be able to be removed once that
restriction is lifted.

classes.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.converter.ConvertVisitor

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.converter.ConvertVisitor.convert
documentation:
Convert a program node into a {class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit`.

If given, `source` is a string containing the OpenQASM 3 source code that was parsed into
`node`. This is used to generated improved error messages. A {class}`.State` containing
information about the conversion is returned. The {class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit` is
stored in property thereof named `circuit`.
This class assumes that the given AST was a valid OpenQASM 3 program.  It is not within our
scope for this simple package to gracefully handle arbitrary semantically invalid programs.
In some places, such as symbol definitions, we do some simple checks to help everyone's
sanity, as the reference package doesn't yet do this.
pylint: disable=missing-function-docstring,no-self-use,unused-argument

methods.name: qiskit_qasm3_import.converter.ConvertVisitor.generic_visit
documentation: Everything below is the implementation of the visitor itself.  The general `visit` method is
derived from the base class.

Internal visitor of an OpenQASM 3 AST to convert it to a
{class}`~qiskit.circuit.QuantumCircuit`. The complete conversion is done by calling
{meth}`convert` on a {class}`openqasm3.ast.Program` node.

The other methods on this class are internal only, and generally not part of the public
interface.
